ap_exam_coverage: Units 1-3 on AP Exam; Unit 4 not assessed
note: All IDs are pool-isolated. No shared IDs with any other pool. AP skill codes reference College Board CED Fall 2023.

params.template: Find the average rate of change of f(x) = {f} on the interval [{a}, {b}].
A polynomial has zeros at x = {z1} (mult {m1}), x = {z2} (mult {m2}), x = {z3} (mult {m3}). Which graph could represent this polynomial?
A degree-{n} polynomial with real coefficients has zeros {given_zeros}. List all remaining zeros.
params.note: Complex zeros come in conjugate pairs for real-coefficient polynomials

params.template: Describe the end behavior of f(x) = {a}x^{n} + ... using limit notation.
params.notation: as x→∞ f(x)→? and as x→-∞ f(x)→?

params.template: Find the horizontal asymptote (or slant asymptote) of f(x) = ({num}) / ({den}).
Find all zeros of f(x) = ({num}) / ({den}). Note any values excluded from domain.
params.note: Zero only if numerator = 0 AND denominator ≠ 0

params.template: Identify all vertical asymptotes of f(x) = ({num}) / ({den}).
Find the location of the hole in f(x) = ({num}) / ({den}).
Rewrite {num}/{den} in the form q(x) + r(x)/{den}.
Describe the transformations from parent function {f}(x) to g(x) = {a}f({b}(x - {h})) + {k}.
Is the sequence {seq} arithmetic, geometric, or neither? If arithmetic, find d. If geometric, find r.
Identify a and b in f(x) = a·b^x given {representation}.
Rewrite {exp_expr} in the form a·b^(cx).
Given f(x) = {f} and g(x) = {g}, find {ask}.
Find f⁻¹(x) for f(x) = {f}. State the domain of f⁻¹.
Evaluate or simplify: {log_expr}
Identify the domain, range, asymptote, and key feature of f(x) = {a}log_b({cx + d}) + {k}.
Solve: {exp_equation}
Solve for x: {log_equation}
Find the exact value of {trig_func}({angle}).
Identify the amplitude, period, and midline of f(x) = {A}sin({B}x) + {D}.
A periodic context: {context}. Write a sinusoidal model f(x) = Asin(Bx + C) + D or Acos(Bx + C) + D.
contexts: Tides: high {max}ft at t={t1}hr, low {min}ft at t={t2}hr
Temperature: max {max}° at hour {t1}, min {min}° at hour {t2}
Ferris wheel: height {max}m at top, {min}m at bottom, period {p} seconds

params.template: Evaluate: {inv_trig_expr}
Convert {point_or_equation} between rectangular and polar form.
Eliminate the parameter t from x={x_t} and y={y_t} to write y as a function of x.
Identify the conic section and write {eq} in standard form.
Given vectors u = <{a},{b}> and v = <{c},{d}>, compute {operation}.
Compute {operation} for matrices A = {A} and B = {B}.

generators.name: Rate of Change: Linear vs Quadratic

params.template: Using the table of values for f, determine if the function is linear, quadratic, or neither. Justify using rates of change.
params.note: 1st differences constant → linear; 2nd differences constant → quadratic

params.template: A quantity {description}. Is this linear or exponential growth? Write the function.
contexts: increases by {d} each year
increases by {r}% each year
doubles every {t} years
params.note: Linear: adds constant; Exponential: multiplies by constant

contexts: Tide height over 12 hours
Temperature over a 24-hour day
Ferris wheel height over time
Sound wave amplitude

params.template: As x increases over {interval}, describe how f(x) behaves.
Write a polynomial with real coefficients, degree {n}, and the given zeros: {zeros}.
Write as a single logarithm: {log_sum_diff}
Solve algebraically, showing all steps: {equation}
Solve on [0, 2π]: {trig_equation}

generators.name: Solve Equations with Inverse Trig — FRQ Style

params.template: Find the exact value of {expression} without a calculator.
params.note: Watch restricted domain of inverse trig functions

contexts: Population grows from {v1} to {v2} thousand between years {t1} and {t2}.
Revenue changes from ${v1}M to ${v2}M between quarters {t1} and {t2}.
Temperature drops from {v1}° to {v2}° between hours {t1} and {t2}.

params.template: The function h(x) is defined below. Sketch or identify key features after applying transformation {T}.
Rewrite {expr} in the form a·e^(kx).
f(x) = {f}, g(x) = {g}. Find the domain of f(g(x)).
A sinusoidal model has max value {max} at t={t_max} and min value {min} at t={t_min}. Find A, B, C, D for f(t) = Asin(Bt+C)+D.

generators.name: Concavity of Sinusoidal Intervals
generators.frq_alignment: FRQ 3: Modeling Periodic Context Part C

params.template: For f(x) = {A}sin({B}x) + {D}, on what intervals is the function concave up? Concave down?
params.note: Concave up = sin-like on (π, 2π); concave down = sin-like on (0, π)

params.template: Three functions are shown: f (graph), g (table), h (equation). Answer Parts (A)–(C).

parts.task: identify appropriate function type for a model

contexts: Scatter plot shows rapid initial growth that levels off
Data shows consistent percent increase per year
Data has a clear maximum and symmetric decline
Rate of change increases then decreases

generators.name: Model Construction — Build System of Equations

params.template: A data set has known values at t={t1} and t={t2}: f({t1})={v1}, f({t2})={v2}. Construct an exponential (or polynomial) model.
Data is plotted on a semi-log plot. The linear regression is log(y) = {m}x + {b}. Write the exponential function y = f(x).
Matrix T = {T} represents a transformation. Apply T to vector/polygon and describe geometric effect.
For f(x) = {rational_function}, identify: zeros, vertical asymptotes, horizontal asymptote, holes, end behavior.

contexts: Population P(t) = {a}·{b}^t. Find P at t={t}, interpret rate.
Investment A(t) = 1000·(1.06)^t. When does it double?
Radioactive decay: Q(t) = 200·(0.5)^(t/30). Half-life interpretation.

params.template: Find the reference angle for {theta} radians. Then find the exact value of {trig_fn}({theta}).
Given sin(θ) = {val} and θ is in Quadrant {Q}, find cos(θ) and tan(θ) without a calculator.
Rewrite {expr} in an equivalent form useful for identifying {feature}.

generators.name: Log Function Transformation — Write Equation from Graph

params.template: A logarithmic function passes through {points} and has vertical asymptote x = {h}. Write the equation.

generators.name: Sinusoidal Rate of Change — FRQ 3 Part C
generators.frq_alignment: FRQ 3: Modeling Periodic Context Part C

params.template: For the sinusoidal model f(t) = {A}sin({B}t + {C}) + {D}: On the interval ({t1}, {t2}), is the rate of change of f positive or negative? Is the rate of change increasing or decreasing?
A polynomial has {n} turning points. What is the minimum possible degree?
A geometric sequence has first term {a1} and common ratio {r}. Find the {n}th term.
Convert the polar equation {r_eq} to rectangular form.

generators.name: Rate of Change Sign and Concavity Interpretation

params.template: The ARC of f on [{a},{b}] is {val}. What does this tell you about f on this interval?
Verify that f and g are inverses by showing f(g(x)) = x AND g(f(x)) = x.

generators.name: Write Sinusoidal Equation from Graph
generators.frq_alignment: FRQ 3: Modeling Periodic Context Part B

params.template: A graph shows a sinusoidal function with max {max} at x = {x_max}, min {min}, and period {T}. Write f(x) = Asin(Bx+C)+D.
Complete: As x→∞, f(x) = {poly} → ___. As x→−∞, f(x) → ___.
Rewrite {expression} in an equivalent form without trig or inverse trig functions, given {constraint}.
